description:
Two warnings in src/ir_codegen.rs that may signal real bugs rather than dead code. Investigate FIRST, before any deletions, so evidence isn't lost.

## 1. signed_b unused at ir_codegen.rs:620

In fz_bs_write_field:
```
let signed_b = signed != 0;  // computed, never used
```

The READ path (fz_bs_read_field, line ~831) uses signed_b at line 886 for sign extension. The WRITE path silently ignores the signed flag.

Action: verify whether writing a signed integer differs from writing an unsigned integer of the same width in our bitstring encoding. In two's-complement at the same width it shouldn't — the bit pattern is identical. If so, drop the binding with a one-line comment explaining why we don't branch. If signed encoding differs (e.g., we emit a sign bit at a different position), restore the missing handling.

## 2. fn_ids dead parameter at ir_codegen.rs:1920

```
fn compile_fn(
    jmod: &mut JITModule,
    ...
    fn_ids: &HashMap<u32, FuncId>,   // never read in body
    ...
```

Check git log on this signature. Likely the value moved into another struct (RuntimeRefs?) and the param was orphaned during a refactor. Either remove the parameter from the signature (and all callers), or restore its intended use site.

## Deliverable

One small commit per investigation (or one combined commit if both turn out to be trivial drops). Net -2 warnings; possibly a code fix if the signed-write path is wrong.
title: Real-bug investigation: signed_b drop + fn_ids dead param in ir_codegen
